name: DocFork Library Documentation MCP Server
what_it_does: Official MCP server from DocFork (docfork org) providing @latest documentation and code examples for 9,000+ libraries — similar to Context7 but from DocFork. Enables AI coding agents to fetch up-to-date library documentation, API references, and usage examples, ensuring agents use current rather than outdated training-data-era API signatures.
use_cases: Fetching current library documentation for coding assistant agents
Getting up-to-date API references for rapidly-changing libraries from AI code editors
Resolving library version compatibility questions from architecture agents
Providing coding agents with accurate, current usage examples for 9000+ packages
not_for: Non-library or general web documentation search
Private/internal API documentation (covers public libraries only)
Libraries not yet indexed in DocFork's catalog
best_when: A coding agent needs accurate, up-to-date documentation for popular libraries — avoiding the common problem of LLMs generating code against outdated or deprecated API signatures.
avoid_when: You need very niche or private library documentation. DocFork covers popular public packages — verify coverage for your specific libraries.

auth.notes: DocFork API key required. Register at docfork.com for access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier available; check docfork.com for current limits
pricing.notes: Free tier available. MCP server is free open source from official docfork org.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limited by DocFork API tier

known_agent_gotchas: Coverage of 9000+ libraries is broad but verify your specific libraries are indexed before relying on this
Documentation freshness depends on DocFork's indexing cadence — check for libraries with rapid release cycles
Official from docfork org — purpose-built for up-to-date library docs
Competing with Context7 for this space — evaluate both for your library coverage needs
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS. API key auth. Public library documentation — non-sensitive. Official DocFork implementation.

security_score_components.security_notes: Public documentation data. HTTPS. API key auth. Low security risk.
